apps.name: Provenance
apps.one_sentence_promise: Photograph what you collect and get insurance-grade condition, provenance, and value tracking without a spreadsheet.
apps.target_user: Serious collectors of high-value physical goods (watches, vinyl, trading cards, wine, sneakers) who currently track holdings in Notes or spreadsheets and have no real sense of aggregate value or condition history.
apps.painful_moment: You just bought or received a piece, you want to log it before the details slip your mind, and existing apps are single-category or spreadsheet-grade.
apps.why_different: Cross-category, condition-aware valuation over time with photographic condition history, and an actual exportable report insurers/appraisers can use.
apps.why_users_pay: Ongoing market-value tracking and alerts, unlimited items/categories, and insurance-ready PDF export are gated behind the paid tier.
apps.subscription_model: Free: manual catalog up to 25 items, no valuation tracking. Paid (~$6.99/mo or $59/yr): unlimited items, on-device photo-based item ID assist, value-over-time charts, insurance export.
apps.local_first_mvp_scope: SwiftData-backed local catalog, on-device Vision framework classification assist, manual condition/provenance fields, local value-history entries, PDF export via local rendering.
apps.future_backend_roadmap: Repository protocol boundary now; later, optional cloud sync and an opt-in market-price feed behind the same interface.
apps.ar_ml_llm_fit: On-device Vision-based item ID assist is integral to reducing cataloging friction; fully usable via manual entry if classification fails.
apps.design_direction: Quiet, archival, museum-catalog aesthetic — dark neutral backgrounds, serif accents, photo-forward cards.
apps.claude_design_prompt: Design a premium iOS collector's valuation app called Provenance. Archival/museum-catalog aesthetic, dark neutral background, serif display type, photo-forward item cards, condition timeline view, value-over-time chart screen. Include empty state, item detail screen, insurance-export preview.
apps.app_store_positioning: The valuation ledger for people who collect things worth tracking.
key_risks: On-device classification may misidentify niche items
Value tracking depends on user-entered market data absent a live price feed
apps.invalidation_criteria: If users only ever use manual entry and value fields go unfilled, reposition as pure catalog+condition tracking.
apps.v1_build_scope: Single-user local catalog, photo capture + classification assist, condition/provenance logging, manual value entries with history chart, PDF insurance export, full state coverage, StoreKit 2 paywall.

apps.name: Scope Guard
apps.one_sentence_promise: Paste in client messages and get scope-creep flagged with a drafted change order before you do unpaid work.
apps.target_user: Independent freelancers and small consultancies who lose money to informal scope creep buried in message threads.
apps.painful_moment: A client casually asks for 'one more small thing' and the freelancer must decide, under time pressure, whether that's billable.
apps.why_different: Intervenes before the unpaid work happens, at the moment the ask lands, instead of tracking time after the fact.
apps.why_users_pay: Unlimited projects/clients and unlimited LLM extractions versus a capped free tier.
apps.subscription_model: Free: 1 active project, 10 extractions/month. Paid (~$9.99/mo): unlimited projects and extractions, change-order templates, PDF export.
apps.local_first_mvp_scope: Local project/client records, pasted-text ingestion, on-device LLM extraction of deliverables vs. new asks, manual scope baseline, change-order draft generation, local export.
apps.future_backend_roadmap: Repository protocol boundary now; later, optional email/Slack ingestion via cloud connector behind the same interface.
core_workflows: LLM flags in-scope vs out-of-scope asks
apps.ar_ml_llm_fit: LLM extraction is the entire value proposition; ships an explicit non-LLM manual-tagging fallback for ineligible devices.
apps.design_direction: Sharp, contract-grade, no-nonsense — legal document meets modern productivity app.
apps.claude_design_prompt: Design a premium iOS app called Scope Guard for freelancers to catch client scope creep. Sharp contract-grade aesthetic, clear in-scope/flagged distinction, paste-in-message screen, review/flag screen, change-order draft preview. Include empty state and an on-device-AI-unavailable fallback state.
apps.app_store_positioning: Stop doing unpaid work you didn't agree to.
key_risks: Devices without Apple Intelligence eligibility need a genuinely usable manual fallback
Extraction quality needs validation against messy real-world phrasing
apps.invalidation_criteria: If freelancers stop pasting messages after week one, or flags are routinely wrong, the mechanic needs rework.
apps.v1_build_scope: Single-user local project/client management, manual scope baseline, paste-and-extract with LLM and manual fallback, change-order draft + export, full state coverage, StoreKit 2 paywall.

apps.name: Practice Loop
apps.one_sentence_promise: Play your instrument and get real pitch and rhythm-stability feedback on the actual piece you're practicing, not just a metronome click.
apps.target_user: Serious amateur and pre-professional instrumentalists who practice alone without a teacher present to catch intonation and timing drift.
apps.painful_moment: Practicing a difficult passage alone and not being sure if it's actually improving.
apps.why_different: Analyzes practice rather than merely timing it — piece-specific pitch/rhythm feedback instead of generic metronome and streak mechanics.
apps.why_users_pay: Unlimited pieces, historical trend analysis, and per-passage breakdowns versus a single-piece free tier.
apps.subscription_model: Free: 1 tracked piece, basic timer + log. Paid (~$7.99/mo or $59/yr): unlimited pieces, on-device audio analysis feedback, progress trends.
apps.local_first_mvp_scope: Local practice-session log, on-device audio capture and analysis (pitch tracking, tempo-stability detection), per-piece history and trend view.
apps.future_backend_roadmap: Repository protocol boundary now; later, optional cloud backup and teacher-sharing of progress reports.
core_workflows: start a practice session for a piece
record and get real-time pitch/rhythm feedback
track progress per piece over time
ios_native_features: On-device audio analysis (AVAudioEngine, no cloud call)
Live Activities during an active session
apps.ar_ml_llm_fit: On-device audio pitch/rhythm analysis is the core value driver, not a bonus — works fully offline.
apps.design_direction: Calm studio aesthetic — warm wood/brass tones, waveform-driven visuals.
apps.claude_design_prompt: Design a premium iOS music-practice app called Practice Loop. Calm studio aesthetic, live waveform and pitch-accuracy visualization during recording, per-piece progress history, session summary screen. Include empty state and a low-signal/no-input error state.
apps.app_store_positioning: The practice partner that actually listens.
key_risks: Analysis accuracy varies by instrument family
Ambient noise could degrade analysis and needs graceful handling
apps.invalidation_criteria: If real instrumentalists find feedback inaccurate, rework before the paid tier leans on it.
apps.v1_build_scope: Single-user local practice log, on-device audio analysis for one or two instrument families at launch, per-piece history/trends, full state coverage, StoreKit 2 paywall.

apps.name: Proof
apps.one_sentence_promise: Log what a batch actually costs you in materials and time, and know the price that keeps you from secretly losing money.
apps.target_user: Side-hustle makers and small-batch sellers who price intuitively and have never calculated true per-unit cost including their own labor time.
apps.painful_moment: Finishing a batch of product and having no real answer to 'am I actually making money on this.'
apps.why_different: Calculates true landed cost per unit including labor at a user-set rate, and derives a defensible minimum price.
apps.why_users_pay: Unlimited products, a reusable material cost library, and profit-trend reporting versus a single-product free tier.
apps.subscription_model: Free: 1 tracked product. Paid (~$5.99/mo or $44/yr): unlimited products, shared material cost library, batch profit-trend history.
apps.local_first_mvp_scope: Local product/recipe records, reusable material cost entries, labor time entry with user-set hourly rate, per-batch true-cost calculation, suggested pricing, local profit-history tracking.
apps.future_backend_roadmap: Repository protocol boundary now; later, optional cloud backup/sync and a marketplace sales-import point behind the same interface.
core_workflows: define a product/recipe with materials and quantities
get a suggested minimum price for a target margin
ios_native_features: Share sheet export of a priced product card
apps.ar_ml_llm_fit: None — deliberately no bonus tech; the value is disciplined cost accounting done well.
apps.design_direction: Warm, craft-market aesthetic with honest, ledger-like data presentation.
apps.claude_design_prompt: Design a premium iOS small-batch-business costing app called Proof. Warm craft-market aesthetic, ledger-like clarity, product/recipe list, materials-and-labor entry screen, suggested-pricing result with profit-trend chart. Include empty state and a state where labor rate hasn't been set yet.
apps.app_store_positioning: The true cost of what you make, finally in plain numbers.
key_risks: Users may resist entering honest labor time, undermining the core calculation
Material entry could feel tedious for makers with many small inputs
apps.invalidation_criteria: If users stop logging labor time within the first few batches, the promise collapses to materials-only and needs repositioning.
apps.v1_build_scope: Single-user local product/recipe and cost tracking, labor-time entry, true-cost calculation with suggested pricing, profit-trend history, full state coverage, StoreKit 2 paywall.

apps.name: Returnwise
apps.one_sentence_promise: Never lose money to missed return windows or forgotten warranties again.
apps.target_user: Frequent online and in-store shoppers who buy enough that return deadlines and warranty terms become easy to miss.
apps.painful_moment: The user realizes an item is wrong or broken and cannot quickly tell whether they can still return or claim it.
apps.why_different: Focuses on deadline leverage and proof readiness, not generic receipt storage or expense tracking.
apps.why_users_pay: Directly saves money by surfacing deadlines, documents, and next actions before options expire.
apps.subscription_model: Free tier for a small number of tracked purchases; premium for unlimited tracking, smart reminders, warranty folders, and return evidence exports.
apps.local_first_mvp_scope: Receipt scan/import, purchase records, deadline calculations, reminder schedules, warranty docs, exportable return packets on-device.
apps.future_backend_roadmap: Email receipt sync, merchant integrations, household sharing, optional cross-device sync.
apps.ar_ml_llm_fit: ML/OCR is integral for extracting merchant, date, and totals from receipts; no LLM dependency required.
apps.design_direction: Sharp, deadline-aware interface with strong calendar cues and clear status labeling.
apps.claude_design_prompt: Design a polished iPhone utility that turns receipts into deadline-driven action cards with calm but urgent timeline cues and clean document handling.
apps.app_store_positioning: A premium receipt and warranty app built around action, not archiving.
key_risks: OCR quality and correction UX must be strong enough for trust
Could slide toward generic expense tracking if return actions are weak
apps.invalidation_criteria: If users still need to manually read every receipt to know what to do, the product isn't differentiated enough.
apps.v1_build_scope: Local purchase tracking with receipt scan/import, editable extracted fields, deadline engine, reminder center, exportable proof bundle, onboarding, paywall, tests.

apps.name: VerveCoach
apps.one_sentence_promise: Rehearse the conversations that matter before you walk into the room.
apps.target_user: Managers, founders, interview candidates, salespeople, and professionals who need to prepare for emotionally or financially important conversations.
apps.painful_moment: The user has one difficult conversation coming up and knows improvising will go badly.
apps.why_different: Rehearsal-driven, not a generic chat assistant or note pad; the product is about practicing responses under pressure.
apps.why_users_pay: Better communication changes outcomes in interviews, negotiations, feedback conversations, and sales calls.
apps.subscription_model: Free limited rehearsal scenarios; premium for unlimited sessions, custom scenario libraries, progress history, advanced coaching modes.
apps.local_first_mvp_scope: Scenario setup, role-based prompt cards, rehearsal flows, reflection notes, session history, optional on-device critique where available.
apps.future_backend_roadmap: Cross-device sync, shared coaching templates, mentor review, richer speech analysis.
core_workflows: Review weak spots and next-best phrasing
ios_native_features: App Intents for quick rehearsal starts
apps.ar_ml_llm_fit: An on-device LLM is useful for critique and response variation, but the app ships strong rule-based rehearsal flows and fallbacks.
apps.design_direction: Confident, minimal, psychologically safe with low-friction rehearsal cards and strong focus states.
apps.claude_design_prompt: Design an iPhone rehearsal app for difficult conversations with calm focus, strong voice interaction, scenario setup, and premium coaching feedback surfaces.
apps.app_store_positioning: A private rehearsal app for people who need better outcomes from the conversations that matter.
key_risks: LLM dependency must not become a device-eligibility trap
Could drift into generic self-help if rehearsal structure is weak
apps.invalidation_criteria: If the app is just a prompt box asking an AI what to say, it fails.
apps.v1_build_scope: Local scenario rehearsal app with manual setup, guided response practice, audio capture, review notes, optional model-backed critique where supported, onboarding, paywall, persistence.

apps.name: CueKeeper
apps.one_sentence_promise: Run a live event from your iPhone without losing the sequence, timing, or backup plan.
apps.target_user: Wedding planners, stage managers, DJs, MCs, and small production operators running events without expensive show-control software.
apps.painful_moment: The event is live, timing is slipping, and someone needs a reliable run-of-show tool that still works offline.
apps.why_different: Designed for live execution under pressure, not static planning or generic to-do lists.
apps.why_users_pay: A single avoided live-event mistake is worth the subscription for professionals and serious operators.
apps.subscription_model: Free for simple single-event runs; premium for unlimited events, cue templates, contingency plans, advanced timing features.
apps.local_first_mvp_scope: Event timelines, cue lists, role assignments, timing controls, contingency notes, offline run mode on-device — scoped to a single operator, not a synced team.
apps.future_backend_roadmap: Team sync, remote cue sharing, client-facing run sheets, multi-device coordination — explicitly deferred, not required for v1 value.
core_workflows: Run cues in live mode with timing support
Adjust and recover when the plan shifts
ios_native_features: App Intents for quick event start
apps.ar_ml_llm_fit: No LLM, AR, or ML required; the product wins on execution reliability.
apps.design_direction: High-contrast operational UI that stays elegant but favors legibility and fast recovery under pressure.
apps.claude_design_prompt: Design a premium live event operations app with clock-aware run mode, strong cue hierarchy, big-touch controls, graceful contingency handling.
apps.app_store_positioning: An offline-first run-of-show app for people who cannot afford chaos during live events.
key_risks: Must feel faster and more trustworthy than a printed run sheet
Live mode interaction design has to be extremely clear
Must stay genuinely useful as a single-operator tool, not silently assume multi-device coordination
apps.invalidation_criteria: If the live run mode is not obviously better than Notes or a PDF schedule, the app should be cut.
apps.v1_build_scope: Single-user event runner with timeline builder, cue execution mode, timers, contingency notes, templates, onboarding, paywall, accessibility, offline verification.
